engineGuide.h2: Mercedes-Benz B Class Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes-Benz B Class across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L / 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: The OM651 is one of Mercedes-Benz‘s most widely used four-cylinder diesel engines, powering high-mileage B Class models with proven durability but known bottom-end weaknesses.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear and guide rail failure (documented in Mercedes Service Campaign 2015/009)
Injector coil pack failure and seal leaks (TSB 07.00/123)
Bearing and piston wear limiting engine lifespan
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651.901 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: The higher-output variant of the OM651 family, fitted to performance-oriented diesel B Class models with 4MATIC optional, delivering 177 hp from the four-cylinder common-rail unit.
commonFailures: DPF and EGR cooler blockage from short-trip driving
Turbocharger failure in high-mileage units
DPF emissions compliance issues affecting MoT pass rate
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651.930 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The clean, efficient OM654q engine introduced with the W247 B Class, tested to RDE-2 emissions standards and paired with 8G-DCT automatic transmission.
compatibleVariants: B180d, B200d, B200d Sport, B200d AMG Line, B220d AMG Line (W247)
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking under thermal stress (A6540940215)
AdBlue system faults and emissions compliance issues
Turbo actuator failure across all diesel variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM654.920 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The original diesel engine fitted to first-generation W245 B Class models, providing efficient motoring for the compact MPV with proven mechanical simplicity.
commonFailures: Injector failure in high-mileage units
Turbocharger bearing wear and failure
EGR valve clogging from carbon buildup
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM640.940 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: A turbocharged four-cylinder with direct injection and BlueDIRECT technology, featuring piezo injectors and multi-spark ignition for efficiency and response from just 1250rpm.
commonFailures: Timing chain wear and tensioner failure (A2700500408)
Camshaft adjuster faults and camshaft fracture risk (particularly 2015 models)
Camshaft bearing wear leading to noise and performance loss
Carbon buildup on intake valves from direct injection
entries.cta: Get quotes for M270.910 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The higher-displacement version of the M270 family, delivering 211 hp for the range-topping B250 with the same turbocharged direct-injection architecture.
compatibleVariants: B250, B250 Sport, B250 AMG Line (W246)
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump failure
Timing chain guide wear affecting all variants
Oil consumption from worn piston rings or turbo seals
entries.cta: Get quotes for M270.920 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.7L / 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The naturally aspirated four-cylinder petrol engine that powered first-generation W245 B Class models, offering simple mechanical design but known oil consumption issues.
commonFailures: Excessive oil consumption
VVT solenoid clogging across all variants
Timing chain stretch in higher-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for M266.960 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: A Renault-derived turbo petrol engine introduced with the W247 generation, replacing the M270 in entry-level models with improved efficiency and CO2 emissions from 125g/km.
compatibleVariants: B180, B200, B180 Sport, B200 AMG Line (W247)
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks
Carbon buildup on intake valves
Turbocharger actuator faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for M282.914 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol / Electric
entries.history: The B250e plug-in hybrid combined the 1.3-litre M282 petrol engine with an electric motor for 218 hp, offering EV-only driving alongside petrol range.
compatibleVariants: B250e, B250e AMG Line, B250e Sport (W247)
commonFailures: Charging system failure and high-voltage battery degradation
Hybrid control unit malfunctions
Electric motor bearing wear
entries.cta: Get quotes for B250e Mercedes-Benz B Class hybrid engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes-Benz B Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mercedes-Benz B Class is broadly reliable, but specific engine families - particularly the diesel OM651 and petrol M270/M266 units - have well-documented failure points that can lead to costly repairs or complete engine replacement. Below we break down the most common B Class engine problems, what they cost to fix at a dealer versus a specialist, and when replacement is the smarter financial decision. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz B Class Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz B Class Timing Chain Failure - Why OM651 & M270 Petrol Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner loses hydraulic pressure, allowing chain slack and guide rail fracture. On OM651 diesels, the chain is located at the rear of the engine (gear side), requiring gearbox removal. On M270 petrol units, chain stretch and camshaft adjuster wear combine to cause rattle and eventual valve timing errors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides, and camshaft adjusters (petrol). No engine removal.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles if caught early. Suitable only before chain jumps or guides fail.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Chain Failure With Valve Damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace bent valves, guides, full timing set, head gasket.
repairOptions.longevity: Engine retains original block. Labour-intensive. Viable if no piston or bore damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of fully rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan. Best value when damage extends to pistons, bearings, or cylinder head.

problems.recommendation: On a B Class worth £3,000-£6,000, a full dealer repair exceeding £4,000 rarely makes sense. If your OM651 or M270 engine is rattling on cold start but still running smoothly, a specialist preventative chain replacement (£900-£1,400) is excellent value. If the chain has already jumped and valves are bent, compare rebuilt engine prices - a specialist fitted unit at £2,800-£4,200 often costs less than a dealer head rebuild and delivers a fresh warranty.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM651 & M270 engine prices for your Mercedes-Benz B Class

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz B Class OM651 Injector Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz B Class OM651 Injector Failure - Diesel Misfire & Hard Start
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz B Class B180 CDI, B200 CDI, B220 CDI (2011-2018, OM651.901 & OM651.930)
problems.rootCause: Injector coil pack failure, seal leakage, or carbon buildup around the injector nozzle causes misfire, rough idle, excessive smoke, and hard starting when hot. OM651 injectors are known for copper seal leaks that produce the distinctive "diesel knock" on cold start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector with genuine or OEM-spec unit. Relearn via diagnostics.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Suitable when only one injector fails and others test within spec.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors, copper seals, leak-off lines. Full coding and adaptation.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k+ miles. Best value when multiple injectors failing or high mileage.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine With Upgraded Injectors
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new injectors, timing components, and warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan. Only recommended if engine has other failures (timing, turbo, bearings).

problems.recommendation: Single injector failure is common on OM651 engines beyond 80,000 miles. A specialist replacement at £350-£500 is straightforward. However, if you're experiencing hard start when hot plus misfire and smoke, multiple injectors are likely failing - a full set replacement at £1,100-£1,600 is more cost-effective than repeated single swaps. Only consider engine replacement if injector failure has caused bore wash or the engine has other major issues.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM651 injector replacement and rebuilt engines for your B Class

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz B Class DPF & EGR Cooler Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz B Class DPF & EGR Cooler Failure - Emissions & Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz B Class B180 CDI, B200 CDI, B220 CDI (2011-2018, OM651.901 & OM651.930); B180d, B200d (2019-Present, OM654.920)
problems.rootCause: On OM651 engines, the EGR cooler cracks internally (coolant loss into exhaust, white smoke, blocked DPF). The DPF clogs from short-trip driving that prevents passive regeneration. On OM654 engines, AdBlue system faults and EGR cooler thermal stress cause emissions warnings and reduced power.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cracked EGR cooler, flush coolant, reset adaptations.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Essential first step if white smoke and coolant loss present.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Professional DPF off-car clean or replacement, new EGR valve/cooler, forced regeneration.
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles post-clean. Suitable when DPF is clogged but not melted or cracked.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine With Upgraded Emissions Components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new EGR cooler, DPF, AdBlue system (OM654), 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ mile lifespan. Required when DPF is melted, turbo damaged, or engine has multiple failures.

problems.recommendation: On a B Class beyond 80,000 miles, replacing a cracked EGR cooler at a specialist (£550-£850) is essential to prevent DPF blockage and turbo damage. If your DPF is clogged but not melted, an off-car professional clean (£700-£1,100) restores function. However, if the car has been driven in limp mode for extended periods, the DPF and turbo may be beyond repair - at that stage, a rebuilt engine with new emissions components is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare OM651 & OM654 rebuilt engine prices with DPF and EGR replacements

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz B Class M270 Petrol Oil Consumption & Camshaft Wear
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz B Class M270 Petrol Oil Consumption & Camshaft Wear
problems.rootCause: The M270 turbo petrol engine suffers from camshaft bearing wear, camshaft adjuster failure, and oil consumption from worn piston rings or turbo seals. Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection) exacerbates drivability issues. The 1.6-litre variant (M270.910) is particularly prone to camshaft fracture.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace camshafts, adjusters, timing chain if stretched. No engine removal.
repairOptions.longevity: 50k-70k miles. Suitable if oil consumption is minimal and bores are healthy.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Top End Rebuild With Valve Cleaning
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head rebuild, new camshafts, adjusters, valves, intake carbon blasting.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Required if camshaft has worn through bearings or valves damaged.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine With Upgraded Internals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded camshafts, timing components, piston rings, 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k+ miles. Best value when oil consumption exceeds 1L/1000 miles or bottom end worn.

problems.recommendation: The M270 engine responds well to early intervention. If you hear camshaft rattle or see the engine management light for camshaft correlation, a specialist camshaft and adjuster replacement (£1,000-£1,600) saves the engine. Once oil consumption exceeds 1 litre per 1,000 miles, piston rings or turbo seals are failing - a rebuilt engine with upgraded internals (£2,500-£3,800 fitted) is more cost-effective than chasing individual repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M270 engine prices for your Mercedes-Benz B Class petrol

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz B Class OM654 AdBlue System Failure (W247 Diesel)
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz B Class OM654 AdBlue System Failure (W247 Diesel)
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz B Class B180d, B200d, B220d (2019-Present, OM654.920)
problems.rootCause: The AdBlue system (SCR - Selective Catalytic Reduction) is prone to pump failure, crystallised injector nozzles, NOx sensor failure, and control module faults. When the system detects a fault, the car enters a "countdown" mode limiting starts and power. OM654 engines are also subject to EGR cooler cracking (coolant loss, white smoke).

repairOptions.tier: Minor - AdBlue Injector or Pump Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty AdBlue injector or pump, reset system, perform teach-in.
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-50k miles. Suitable for single component failure without wider system damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace pump, injector, NOx sensors, flush tank, update software.
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-60k miles. Required when multiple components fail or tank crystallisation occurs.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine With New Emissions System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new AdBlue system, EGR cooler, DPF, 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k+ miles. Only if engine has additional failures or car out of warranty with chronic AdBlue issues.

problems.recommendation: The OM654 is otherwise a strong engine, but AdBlue system failures are frustratingly common. If your B Class is under manufacturer warranty, insist on a full system replacement. Out of warranty, a specialist single-component fix (£350-£600) is the sensible first step. However, if you have repeated AdBlue faults, crystallisation in the tank, and NOx sensor failures, a specialist rebuilt engine with a brand-new emissions system (£3,200-£4,800 fitted) eliminates the headache - and you get a 12‑month warranty on the entire unit.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM654 engine prices for your Mercedes-Benz B Class (W247)

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz B Class OM640 Turbocharger & Injector Failure (W245 Diesel)
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz B Class OM640 Turbocharger & Injector Failure (W245 Diesel)
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz B Class B180 CDI, B200 CDI (2005-2011, OM640.940)
problems.rootCause: The first-generation OM640 diesel suffers from turbocharger bearing wear (blue smoke, loss of power) and injector seal leaks (diesel knock, hard starting). The injector failure is often the precursor to piston ring damage and bore wash. These engines are mechanically simple but age-related fatigue is now common as W245 models exceed 15 years old.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbocharger with OEM-spec unit, new gaskets, oil change, diagnostics.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Suitable if engine otherwise healthy and injectors tested good.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors and turbocharger, new seals, full adaptation.
repairOptions.longevity: 60k-80k miles. Best value when both components failing (common beyond 120k miles).

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine With Upgraded Components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new injectors, turbo, timing components, 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k+ miles. Recommended if bore wash from leaking injectors has damaged piston rings or bores.

problems.recommendation: W245 B Class values are now £1,500-£3,500. A dealer full replacement exceeding £5,500 is rarely justified. If your OM640 engine has turbo failure and injector knock, compare a specialist fitted rebuilt engine at £2,400-£3,400 - you get a fresh turbo, new injectors, and a 12-month warranty for less than a dealer turbo-plus-injector repair. Only pursue component-level repairs if the car is exceptionally clean and low-mileage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM640 engine prices for your Mercedes-Benz B Class (W245)

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes-Benz B Class
finalCta.paragraph: Engine failure doesn't have to mean the end of the road for your B Class. Whether you're facing timing chain rattle on an OM651, AdBlue nightmares on an OM654, or oil consumption on an M270 petrol, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mercedes-Benz B Class specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices today, choose a vetted specialist, and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes-Benz B Class and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes-Benz B Class Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes-Benz B Class is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes verified from UK market data.

groups.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre OM607 four-cylinder diesel was co-developed with Renault (badged 1.5 dCi) and fitted to entry-level B Class models from 2014 onward. This EU5/EU6 compliant unit prioritised fuel economy over performance and became the subject of a major Mercedes emissions software update affecting several hundred thousand UK cars in 2017-2018 .
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre four-cylinder diesel (often marketed as 1.8 CDI) served as the entry-level diesel option for early W246 generation B Class models from 2011 to 2014. In B180 CDI form it produced 109bhp, while the B200 CDI variant delivered 136bhp with the same displacement and the OM651 engine architecture .
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.8L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre diesel became the backbone of the B Class diesel range across multiple generations. Early W245 models used the OM640.940 (B200 CDI), while later W246 and W247 models adopted the OM654.920 with AdBlue emissions technology and improved fuel efficiency. The OM654 introduced in 2019 meets RDE-2 standards and is paired with the 8G-DCT transmission .
commonFailurePoints: OM640: turbo bearing wear; OM654: AdBlue system faults
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz B Class 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.1-litre OM651.930 represented the performance-oriented diesel option for the W246 B Class, delivering 177hp in the B220 CDI badge. This engine was also available with 4MATIC all-wheel drive, distinguishing it from smaller diesel variants. The OM651 family became known for timing chain issues and DPF-related failures at higher mileages .
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz B Class 2.1L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3-litre M282 turbocharged four-cylinder arrived with the W247 generation in 2019. Co-developed with Renault, this unit replaced the earlier M270 1.6-litre across entry-level and mid-range B Class petrol variants. It features direct injection, a twin-scroll turbocharger, and cylinder deactivation technology to improve real-world fuel economy .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5-litre petrol engine was available only in the first-generation W245 B Class (2005-2011), serving as the smallest and most economical petrol option in the range. This naturally aspirated four-cylinder unit prioritised urban fuel efficiency over outright performance.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre M270 turbocharged four-cylinder debuted with the 2012 W246 B Class, replacing the older naturally aspirated M266 units. Available in B160 (102hp) and B180 (122hp) states of tune, this engine brought direct injection and turbocharging to the B Class petrol range for the first time. The M270 became known for camshaft and timing chain issues in higher-mileage examples .
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.7-litre M266.960 naturally aspirated engine powered the W245 generation B170 (2005-2011). This unit represented the mid-range petrol option in the original B Class lineup, offering 116hp and simple mechanical design. The M266 family gained a reputation for oil consumption at higher mileages but remains one of the more straightforward B Class engines to service.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre petrol options appeared in two distinct forms across B Class generations. The W245 B200 used a naturally aspirated M266.960 producing 136hp. The W246 B250 adopted the turbocharged M270.920 delivering 211hp, representing the range-topping petrol variant until the B250e plug-in hybrid arrived. The B250's M270.920 is known for high-pressure fuel pump failures and timing chain guide wear .
commonFailurePoints: M266: oil consumption; M270.920: HPFP failure
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class 2.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class MHEV / Hybrid Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.3L MHEV Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3-litre M282 engine was optionally equipped with a 48V mild hybrid system on later W247 models. This belt-driven starter-generator (RSG) provides a 14hp boost during acceleration, enables coasting with the engine off, and delivers smoother stop-start operation. The MHEV system helps reduce fuel consumption and CO2 emissions without requiring external charging .
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.3L MHEV engine availability

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.3L Plug-In Hybrid Engines
items.description: The B250e combined the 1.3-litre M282 petrol engine with an electric motor and a high-voltage battery, delivering 218hp combined. Offered exclusively on the W247 generation from 2019 to 2022, the B250e could travel up to 40 miles on electric power alone before the petrol engine engaged. The hybrid system prioritised EV operation for urban driving and remains a rare but sought-after variant in the used market.
items.cta: Enquire about Mercedes-Benz B Class 1.3L plug-in hybrid engine availability

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class Petrol Engines
items.description: A diverse petrol lineup spanning naturally aspirated simplicity (M266) through turbocharged direct injection (M270) to the latest Renault co-developed M282 unit - each with distinct failure patterns.
items.descriptor: A diverse petrol lineup spanning naturally aspirated simplicity (M266) through turbocharged direct injection (M270) to the latest Renault co-developed M282 unit - each with distinct failure patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes-Benz B Class Petrol Engine Families

items.title: Mercedes-Benz B Class Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The B250e combined the 1.3-litre M282 petrol engine with an electric motor for urban EV capability and petrol range flexibility - offered only on the W247 generation between 2019 and 2022.
items.descriptor: The B250e combined the 1.3-litre M282 petrol engine with an electric motor for urban EV capability and petrol range flexibility - offered only on the W247 generation between 2019 and 2022.
items.familiesLabel: Mercedes-Benz B Class PHEV Engine Codes

items.description: Later W247 generation 1.3-litre petrol models received a 48V belt-driven starter-generator (RSG) system, offering electric boost and coasting functionality without plug-in capability.
items.descriptor: Later W247 generation 1.3-litre petrol models received a 48V belt-driven starter-generator (RSG) system, offering electric boost and coasting functionality without plug-in capability.

engineYears.h2: Mercedes-Benz B Class Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 2005 launch of the first-generation compact MPV to the latest current-generation models, this timeline covers every major engine change across the B Class range - helping you identify your exact unit and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: W245 Launch: M266 Petrol & OM640 Diesel Arrive
years.description: The original Mercedes-Benz B Class (W245) launched with naturally aspirated petrol and diesel engines, prioritising interior space over performance.
keyChanges: New compact MPV platform (W245) with front-wheel drive
Launch engines: M266 petrol (1.5L, 1.7L, 2.0L) and OM640 diesel (2.0L)
Five-speed manual and CVT automatic transmissions offered
B150, B170, B200 petrol; B180 CDI, B200 CDI diesel
knownFor: Clever sandwich floor platform for crash safety and flat load floor
M266 engines are simple but prone to oil consumption beyond 80k miles
OM640 diesel is robust but injector and turbo failures common at high mileage
CVT transmission (Autotronic) has reliability concerns
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2008 Mercedes-Benz B Class replacement engines

years.preview: W245 Facelift: Minor Updates, Same Engines
years.description: The W245 received a mid-cycle refresh with cosmetic updates and interior improvements, but the engine lineup remained unchanged.
keyChanges: Exterior facelift: revised bumpers, lights and grille
Interior updates: new steering wheel, trim options, improved materials
Engine range carried over unchanged from pre-facelift
B170 NGT (Natural Gas Technology) introduced in select markets
knownFor: Late W245 examples are well-sorted and represent good used value
Same engine strengths and weaknesses as early cars
Parts availability remains good across M266 and OM640 families
Some of the last affordable Mercedes-Benz MPVs before W246 price rise
years.cta: Compare 2008-2011 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine prices

years.preview: W246 Launch: OM651 Diesel & M270 Petrol Arrive
years.description: The second-generation W246 B Class arrived with entirely new engine families - the OM651 diesel and M270 turbo petrol - bringing modern turbocharging and direct injection to the range.
keyChanges: New W246 platform: more conventional MPV styling
OM651 diesel family replaces OM640 (1.8L and 2.0L variants)
M270 turbocharged petrol family replaces naturally aspirated M266
B160, B180, B200 petrol (1.6L turbo); B180 CDI, B200 CDI diesel (1.8L)
Six-speed manual and 7G-DCT dual-clutch automatic introduced
knownFor: Major step forward in refinement and fuel economy
OM651 timing chain tensioner and guide rail failures (60k-100k miles)
M270 camshaft wear and timing chain stretch on early cars
7G-DCT transmission generally reliable but requires regular servicing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Mercedes-Benz B Class replacement engines
years.ticker: OM651.901 timing chain replacement B Class • M270.910 camshaft failure B180 • B200 CDI injector failure • W246 B Class engine rattle on cold start

W246 B Class engine rattle on cold start

years.description: Mercedes expanded the W246 range upward with higher-output variants - the B220 CDI diesel and B250 turbo petrol, targeting performance-oriented buyers.
keyChanges: B220 CDI introduced: 2.1L OM651.930 with 177hp
B250 petrol introduced: 2.0L M270.920 with 211hp
4MATIC all-wheel drive optional on B220 CDI
Sport and AMG Line trims become available with these engines
knownFor: B220 CDI offers strong torque and genuine motorway cruiser ability
OM651.930 shares timing chain vulnerabilities with lower-output OM651 variants
B250 petrol is the quickest non-hybrid B Class (0-62 in 6.8 seconds)
Both variants command higher used prices and specialist rebuild costs
years.cta: Compare 2013-2014 Mercedes-Benz B Class performance engine prices

years.description: The W246 facelift brought revised styling and the introduction of the Renault-derived OM607 1.5-litre diesel for entry-level models, alongside continued OM651 and M270 production.
keyChanges: Exterior facelift: LED lights, revised bumpers, new grille
OM607.951 1.5L diesel introduced (B160 CDI / B180 CDI)
OM651.901 continues for B200 CDI
B220 CDI (OM651.930) and B250 (M270.920) remain available
Updated interior with larger infotainment screen
knownFor: OM607 is economical but subject to emissions software updates (2017-2018)
Facelift cars generally have later-revision engines with some improvements
OM651 timing chain issues remain but less frequent on post-2014 builds
Largest spread of engine options of any B Class generation
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2018 Mercedes-Benz B Class replacement engines
years.ticker: OM607 injector failure B180 CDI • OM651 DPF blockage W246 • B250 HPFP failure • B220 CDI turbo replacement cost

years.preview: W247 Launch: OM654 Diesel & M282 Petrol Arrive
years.description: The third-generation W247 B Class moved to a more conventional hatchback design and introduced the clean OM654 diesel and Renault co-developed M282 petrol engines, along with the B250e plug-in hybrid.
keyChanges: New W247 platform: longer, wider, lower than W246
OM654.920 2.0L diesel (B180d / B200d) meets RDE-2 emissions
M282.914 1.3L turbo petrol (B180 / B200) replaces M270
B250e plug-in hybrid (1.3L petrol + electric motor, 218hp)
8G-DCT dual-clutch automatic replaces 7G-DCT
knownFor: OM654 is cleaner and more efficient than OM651 but AdBlue system failure prone
M282 petrol is peppy but suffers injector seal leaks and carbon buildup
B250e offers up to 40 miles electric range - rare and sought-after
W247 represents the most refined B Class to date

48V belt-driven starter-generator (RSG) added to M282 petrol (14hp boost)

Larger MBUX infotainment screens and steering wheel controls

faq.h2: Mercedes-Benz B Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mercedes-Benz B Class engine typically costs £1,500-£3,500 supply-only, depending on engine code (OM651, OM654, M270) and generation. W245 diesels are cheapest; OM654 units for W247 command premium pricing.
keyPoints: Used engines from breakers: £900-£1,800 depending on mileage
Fitting adds £400-£800 at a specialist (not dealer)
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Mercedes-Benz B Class diesel engine reliable?
items.answer: The B200 CDI with OM640 (W245, 2005-2011) and facelift OM651 (W246, 2014-2018) are considered highly reliable, often exceeding 200,000 miles. However, OM651 timing chains and OM654 AdBlue systems have documented weak points.
keyPoints: B200 CDI (2008-2012 facelift) called "gold standard reliability" by specialists
OM651 timing chain tensioner recalls affected Feb-Nov 2014 builds
OM654 AdBlue pump and NOx sensor failures common from 30k miles
W245 CDI engines regularly reach 217,000-280,000 miles with maintenance
OM607 1.5-litre diesel subject to 2017-2018 emissions software updates
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz B Class diesel replacement engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: What are the most common Mercedes-Benz B Class engine problems?
items.answer: OM651 diesel timing chain failure (W246, 60k-100k miles) and M270 petrol camshaft wear (W246, 50k-90k miles) dominate replacement enquiries. OM654 AdBlue system faults affect newer W247 diesels from 30,000 miles.
keyPoints: OM651: timing chain tensioner failure, injector seal leaks, EGR cooler cracking
M270 petrol: camshaft bearing wear, timing chain stretch, HPFP failure
OM654: AdBlue pump, NOx sensor, SDPF degradation
OM640 (W245 diesel): turbo bearing wear, injector failure around 120k miles
P0016 error code indicates camshaft/crankshaft correlation (timing chain stretch)
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Mercedes-Benz B Class engine with upgraded timing components

items.question: Which Mercedes-Benz B Class engine should I avoid?
items.answer: Avoid early W245 petrol compressors (2005-2008 B200 Turbo) due to supercharger clutch and oil consumption issues. Also be cautious with 2011-2013 OM651 diesels before timing chain tensioner recalls and early 2019 OM654 units with AdBlue teething problems.
keyPoints: B200 Turbo (W245 pre-2008): supercharger magnetic clutch failure, heavy oil use
Early OM651 (2011-2013): higher timing chain failure risk before recall improvements
7G-DCT gearbox on 2011-2013 W246: erratic shifts, repair costs up to £3,500
Early W247 OM654 (2019): AdBlue system bugs requiring multiple software updates
CVT transmission on early W245: complete failure at £4,000-£5,000 repair cost
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class engine prices by generation - get the best value

items.question: How long do Mercedes-Benz B Class engines last?
items.answer: Well-maintained OM651 and OM640 diesels regularly reach 200,000-280,000 miles. M270 petrol engines typically last 150,000-180,000 miles with proper servicing. OM654 diesels are too new for definitive data but expected to match OM651 longevity.
keyPoints: OM640/W245 CDI: 217,000-280,000 miles with regular maintenance
OM651: 180,000-250,000 miles if timing chain addressed preventatively
M270 petrol: 150,000-180,000 miles; camshaft wear the main limiter
Facelift (2008+) engines significantly more durable than early units
Short-trip driving kills DPF on diesels - use for longer journeys
items.cta: Get quotes for high-mileage Mercedes-Benz B Class rebuilt engines

items.question: Mercedes-Benz B Class timing chain failure - what causes it and how much to fix?
items.answer: OM651 and M270 engines suffer timing chain tensioner failure (diesel) or chain stretch (petrol). Specialist preventative replacement costs £900-£1,400. Full engine replacement after chain snap costs £2,800-£4,200 fitted.
keyPoints: OM651: tensioner loses hydraulic pressure, chain rattles on cold start
M270: chain stretch and camshaft adjuster wear cause rattle
Preventative chain replacement: £900-£1,400 at specialist
After valve damage: £2,200-£3,200 for head rebuild
Complete rebuilt engine: £2,800-£4,200 fitted with warranty
items.cta: Compare OM651 and M270 timing chain replacement costs for your Mercedes-Benz B Class

items.question: Petrol or diesel Mercedes-Benz B Class - which is better for engine longevity?
items.answer: Diesel engines (OM640, OM651) outlast petrols by 50,000-100,000 miles but have higher repair costs when they fail. Petrol M270 units are cheaper to rebuild but suffer camshaft wear earlier. Choose diesel for high mileage, petrol for urban use.
keyPoints: Diesel: 200k-280k miles possible but timing chain risk
Petrol: 150k-180k miles typical; camshaft wear from 50k miles
Diesel repairs (injectors, DPF, turbo) more expensive than petrol
OM654 diesel AdBlue faults make petrol simpler for low-mileage owners
B200 CDI (136hp facelift) widely considered most reliable variant
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz B Class diesel vs petrol engine replacement prices

items.question: What is the Mercedes-Benz B Class OM654 AdBlue problem?
items.answer: OM654 diesel engines (W247 B180d/B200d, 2019+) suffer AdBlue pump, injector, and NOx sensor failures. Crystallisation blocks the dosing valves, triggering countdown mode with limited starts. Specialist repair costs £350-£1,400; ignoring it can require full engine replacement.
keyPoints: Two-stage AdBlue system (motors near SCR + underbody SCR) doubles failure points
Crystallisation clogs dosing valves - XENTRY diagnostics required
Single component replacement: £350-£600 at specialist
Full AdBlue system overhaul: £900-£1,400
Without repair, countdown leads to immobilised car and potential engine damage
items.cta: Enquire about OM654 Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement with new AdBlue system

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Mercedes-Benz B Class?
items.answer: For W245 models worth £1,500-£3,500, a £2,400-£3,400 rebuilt engine often exceeds the car's value. However, if the body and transmission are sound, specialist replacement with 12-month warranty can be cheaper than replacing the whole vehicle. W246 and W247 models (worth £5k-£15k) are almost always worth repairing.
keyPoints: W245 (2005-2011): value £1.5k-£3.5k - borderline economical
W246 (2011-2018): value £4k-£10k - replacement makes sense
W247 (2019+): value £10k-£18k - definitely worth replacing
Rebuilt engine (£2.4k-£4.2k fitted) cheaper than comparable replacement car
Factory-quality rebuild with upgraded components often outlasts original
items.cta: Get an instant quote for Mercedes-Benz B Class engine replacement - no obligation

items.question: How do I find the correct engine code for my Mercedes-Benz B Class?
items.answer: Check your vehicle's service booklet, door pillar sticker, or V5C logbook for the six-digit engine code (e.g., OM651.901). Enter your registration on EngineMarket above - we instantly identify your exact engine, variant, and compatible replacement options.
keyPoints: Engine code format: three letters + three numbers (OM651.901, M270.910)
Found on driver's side door pillar sticker or V5C section 4
W245 (2005-2011): OM640 (diesel) or M266 (petrol)
W246 (2011-2018): OM607/OM651 (diesel) or M270 (petrol)
W247 (2019+): OM654 (diesel) or M282 (petrol/MHEV/PHEV)
items.cta: Enter your reg now - identify your Mercedes-Benz B Class engine code instantly
